# PR Readiness Checklist: Argo CD Application Onboarding

**Purpose**: Validate that the feature requirements and design artifacts are clear, complete, consistent, and ready for PR task breakdown.
**Created**: 2026-05-24
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command as "unit tests for English"; it validates requirements quality, not implementation behavior.

## Scope Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all 14 requested applications consistently enumerated across the spec, plan, data model, contracts, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Application Matrix, Contract §Dependency Contract, Quickstart §1]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the post-clarification NFS StorageClass requirements reflected in all first-class requirement sources, not only in the spec? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016-FR-021, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §StatefulWorkloadProfile, Contract §Storage Contract, Quickstart §6]
- [ ] CHK003 Are first-rollout exclusions explicitly documented, including no Tailscale Funnel paths and no NFS provisioner ownership? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-012]

## Dependency And Ordering Quality

- [ ] CHK004 Are all app dependency edges specified unambiguously and consistently between the spec and contract, including Tailscale before tailnet reachability and LiteLLM before OpenClaw? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004-FR-010, Contract §Dependency Contract]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the Argo CD bootstrap prerequisite defined clearly enough to distinguish pre-existing bootstrap state from the 14 app onboarding scope? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §Application Matrix]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements defined for what happens when a required upstream app is absent, unhealthy, or cannot be registered without creating circular dependencies? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-004-FR-010]

## Storage And Backup Quality

- [ ] CHK007 Are the requirements clear that the existing NFS provisioner must be discovered by read-only inspection before the default StorageClass desired state is committed? [Clarity, Spec §FR-018-FR-020, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK008 Are unsafe public-repository values for NFS provisioner details defined with enough specificity to guide placeholder or safe-reference decisions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK009 Are NFS backup coverage and restore expectations required before stateful app readiness in both requirements and success criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021, Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK010 Are stateful workload profiles required for every stateful app named in the plan, including Prometheus, Grafana, Tines, Radarr, Sonarr, Deluge, OpenClaw, and LiteLLM? [Coverage, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §6]

## Secret Safety Quality

- [ ] CHK011 Are runtime secret requirements consistently limited to AWS SSM Parameter Store references and ExternalSecret-owned runtime material? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-014, Contract §Secret Reference Contract]
- [ ] CHK012 Are application-specific secret categories named clearly enough for LiteLLM provider keys, Tailscale credentials, app admin credentials, and webhook secrets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Secret Reference Contract]
- [ ] CHK013 Are requirements explicit that secret values, raw certificates, private keys, and kubeconfigs with credentials are excluded from committed desired state? [Clarity, Spec §SC-003, Contract §Secret Reference Contract]

## Networking And Exposure Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Are tailnet-only first-rollout requirements consistent across the spec, plan, ingress contract, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Summary, Contract §Ingress Contract, Quickstart §5]
- [ ] CHK015 Are future Tailscale Funnel webhook exceptions specified as future-only and defined with owner, path, purpose, and rollback requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-023, Contract §Ingress Contract]
- [ ] CHK016 Is the no-DNS-churn requirement measurable and consistently tied to initial DNS setup across the requirements and design artifacts? [Measurability, Spec §FR-009, Spec §SC-011, Contract §Ingress Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Are success criteria measurable enough for PR review, including counts for applications, dependencies, public Funnel paths, secret references, and StorageClass readiness? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-012]
- [ ] CHK018 Are success criteria aligned with rollout gates so stateful applications cannot be considered ready before NFS backup coverage is documented? [Consistency, Spec §FR-021, Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK019 Are Argo CD sync and health expectations specific enough to allow documented exceptions without weakening the 30-minute rollout target? [Clarity, Spec §SC-009]

## Recovery And Rollback Quality

- [ ] CHK020 Are rollback requirements complete for both app dependency order and persistent data preservation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-025, Spec §SC-010, Quickstart §9]
- [ ] CHK021 Are failure and recovery requirements defined for unavailable External Secrets, missing AWS SSM parameters, unavailable Tailscale reachability, and incompatible NFS provisioner state? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK022 Are break-glass or live-state exception requirements clear enough to require repository backfill rather than permanent manual mutation? [Clarity, Spec §IAC-005, AGENTS §Safety rules]

## Traceability And Drift

- [ ] CHK023 Is each major requirement traceable to a design artifact section or is it clearly marked as a spec-only requirement that must be propagated by rerunning planning? [Traceability, Spec §Requirements, Plan §Phase 1, Data Model §Entities, Contract §Terragrunt Unit Contract]
- [ ] CHK024 Are any plan, data model, contract, or quickstart statements stale after the latest NFS clarification, especially statements that assume storage and backup are merely app-local documentation? [Conflict, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §6]

## Notes

- Focus areas: full PR requirements-readiness gate across scope, dependencies, storage, secrets, networking, rollout, and drift.
- Depth: lightweight sanity check.
- Actor and timing: PR reviewer before `/speckit.tasks`.
- Source boundary: spec plus plan, data model, contracts, and quickstart.
